mappings.notes: Exploiting legitimate agent credentials to access AI systems or data pipelines
Using compromised agent credentials to exfiltrate data through inference API
Reconstructing sensitive data accessible to the agent through credential abuse
Agent identity and credential governance policy — NHI inventory, short-lived credentials, scope controls
Agent credential exposure risks mapped per deployment — credential types, scope, and lateral movement potential
Adversarial testing covering credential leakage and abuse — memory stores, logs, tool payload captures
Incident response for credential exposure — rotation, containment, lateral movement assessment, downstream notification

mappings.control_id: Credential exposure risk identified and mitigated
mappings.notes: Agent credential lifecycle in Art. 9 risk management — NHI inventory, scope controls, rotation documented

mappings.control_id: Cybersecurity measures protecting against credential theft
mappings.notes: Short-lived credentials, JIT access, PKI-backed identities are Art. 15 requirements

mappings.control_id: Documented procedures for credential incident response
mappings.notes: Agent credential incident response in quality management system — rotation, containment, lateral movement assessment
Agent credentials managed as privileged access — JIT issuance, minimum scope, regular review, automatic expiry
NHI lifecycle management — all agent identities inventoried, provisioned, reviewed, and deprovisioned through formal process
Agent credentials encrypted at rest and in transit — secret manager, no cleartext storage
All credential operations logged — issuance, use, expiry, anomalous patterns detectable
Credential security as AIMS design requirement — short-lived JIT credentials, no cleartext storage, least privilege
Agent credentials tracked as AI system data — issuance, scope, expiry, rotation documented
Resources for AI systems include NHIs — agent identities inventoried and managed as AIMS resources
Third-party services accessed via agent credentials assessed — security obligations in access arrangements
Agent credentials managed as privileged accounts — minimum scope, regular review, JIT issuance
Agent credential revocation process — immediate revocation on detection, decommission procedure
All credential operations logged — issuance, use, anomalous patterns detectable
Agent credentials encrypted at rest — secret manager, no cleartext storage

mappings.control_name: Verify credentials not in source code
mappings.notes: Agent credentials not hardcoded — secret manager required

mappings.control_name: Verify access control enforces least privilege
mappings.notes: Agent credential scope minimum required — no over-privileged NHIs

mappings.control_name: Verify sensitive data encrypted at rest
mappings.notes: Agent credentials encrypted at rest — no cleartext in config or agent memory
All credential usage logged — issuance, access, expiry detectable

mappings.control_name: Verify secrets not in source code
mappings.notes: Agent credentials not committed to source control
All agent access to OT systems using distinct, traceable identity — not shared service accounts
Agent credentials managed with defined lifecycle — issuance, rotation, revocation procedures
Agent credentials scoped to minimum OT data access required for defined task
Agent credentials never transmitted or stored in cleartext within OT network
Agent sessions terminated and credentials revoked immediately on compromise detection
Memory corruption and state manipulation
Assess agent memory stores as OT data integrity risk
Identities and credentials managed — NHI inventory, lifecycle management, unique identity per agent
Access permissions managed — agent credential scope enforced, least privilege per agent role
Sensitive data protected at rest — agent credentials encrypted, not stored in cleartext
Credential usage monitored — anomalous access patterns detected

mappings.control_id: Agent credentials managed as logical access assets — provisioning, scope, rotation, revocation per access management policy
mappings.notes: Credential lifecycle records, provisioning logs

mappings.control_id: Credentials revoked at session end — no persistent credential caching in agent memory after session terminates
mappings.notes: Session termination logs, credential TTL configuration

mappings.control_id: Credential usage monitored — anomalous scope expansion or after-session access detected
mappings.notes: Credential audit log, anomaly alert records

mappings.control_id: Agent credentials treated as confidential information — stored in secrets manager, access-controlled
mappings.notes: Secrets management configuration, access control evidence

mappings.control_id: Agent accounts are system/application accounts — unique agent identity per deployment, no shared credentials
mappings.notes: Account inventory, unique account evidence

mappings.control_id: Agent credential management — unique credentials, strong authentication where interactive, credential rotation schedule
mappings.notes: Credential management policy, rotation records

mappings.control_id: Agent access to CHD follows need-to-know — access to cardholder data restricted to what agent function requires
mappings.notes: Access control matrix, need-to-know justification

mappings.control_id: Agent credential usage logged — all authentication events for agent accounts in CHD scope logged
mappings.notes: Authentication audit log
Agent credentials treated as sensitive AI data assets — stored in secrets manager, never in agent memory or model context
Agent credential lifecycle policy documented in AI governance framework — scope, lifetime, rotation, revocation
All agent credential operations logged — anomaly detection for scope expansion or after-session access
Short-lived tokens for all agent interactions — credential TTL enforced at the IAM layer
Include memory store read/write paths in threat model
Validate and sanitise all content written to persistent memory
Test persistent memory integrity across session boundaries
Apply access controls, encryption at rest, and integrity verification to all memory stores
Confirm memory stores have appropriate access controls
Agent runs with broader credentials than its task requires; NHI-5 over-privilege
Agent credentials stored in cleartext memory, logs, config, or tool payloads
Credentials and tokens not encrypted at rest in agent memory or configuration
Agent credentials hardcoded in prompts or source code
Long-lived agent credentials without expiry or rotation — NHI-7
Verify agent cannot access systems beyond its credential scope; test scope boundaries
Verify all credential operations are logged with sufficient detail for forensic investigation
Test whether credentials appear in agent outputs, logs, memory stores, or tool payloads

mappings.control_name: Enforce user access privileges to AI systems

mappings.control_id: Agent credentials not revoked on decommission — dormant tokens exploitable
mappings.notes: Formal agent offboarding — all credentials revoked, tokens invalidated, access removed

mappings.control_id: Agent credentials exposed in memory, logs, tool payloads
mappings.notes: No credentials in agent context, logs, or tool payloads — secret manager only

mappings.control_id: Third-party tool credentials with excessive permissions inherited by agent
mappings.notes: Validate all third-party NHIs — revoke over-privileged third-party tokens

mappings.control_id: Weak agent-to-system authentication — credential reuse or weak secrets
mappings.notes: Strong authentication for all agent-to-system connections — mTLS, short-lived tokens

mappings.control_id: Agent holds more permissions than required — lateral movement amplifier
mappings.notes: Least privilege per agent role — scope enforced, reviewed quarterly

mappings.control_id: Agent credentials stored in cleartext — config files, environment variables
mappings.notes: Secret manager for all agent credentials — no cleartext storage anywhere

mappings.control_id: Agent tokens without expiry — compromise persists indefinitely
mappings.notes: Short-lived credentials — task-scoped tokens with automatic expiry

mappings.control_id: Production agent credentials accessible in dev/test environments
mappings.notes: Strict environment isolation — separate credentials per environment, no cross-environment reuse

mappings.control_id: Shared agent credential across multiple instances or deployments
mappings.notes: Unique identity per agent deployment — no credential sharing

mappings.control_id: Humans using agent service accounts — no attribution, no MFA
mappings.notes: Agent credentials machine-only — human use detected and blocked

mappings.control_id: Define explicit privilege boundaries for each agent identity — maximum permitted privilege level, credential scope, and escalation constraints
mappings.notes: Establishes privilege boundaries as mandatory requirements

mappings.control_id: Implement secure credential handling — agents must not inherit user credentials, store tokens in context, or pass credentials between agents without explicit authorisation
mappings.notes: Prevents credential leakage through agent code paths

mappings.control_id: Protect credential stores, identity configurations, and privilege mapping files from unauthorised access and modification
mappings.control_name: PS.1.1-PS – Protect all code from unauthorised access
mappings.notes: Prevents tampering with privilege boundaries

mappings.control_id: Establish monitoring and triage procedures for privilege escalation incidents — detect agents operating beyond their assigned privilege level
mappings.notes: Enables rapid detection of privilege escalation in production
Enforce least privilege with explicit privilege ceilings per agent; prevent accumulation of permissions through tool chaining or inter-agent delegation
Assign unique non-human identities to each AI agent; authenticate agent identity at each tool invocation and inter-agent communication boundary
Enforce access control at every tool invocation boundary; validate agent identity and authorisation for each requested action regardless of calling context
Define incident handling procedures for agent privilege escalation events; include automated containment, privilege revocation, and forensic investigation

mappings.control_name: Protection and Prevention — escalation prevention controls
mappings.notes: Implement security controls preventing agent privilege escalation through tool chaining, inter-agent delegation, or permission accumulation

mappings.control_name: ICT Incident Management — escalation incident reporting
mappings.notes: Classify agent privilege escalation as an ICT-related incident; assess impact on financial systems and report per DORA incident classification criteria
Include agent privilege escalation scenarios in threat-led penetration testing; test for escalation through tool chaining and inter-agent trust
Deploy detection mechanisms for agent privilege escalation; monitor for permission boundary violations and unexpected privilege accumulation
